name: X: Open URL
summary: Open any X URL (post, longform article, or same-author thread) read-only and return a unified markdown rendering plus media + linked-page URLs.
description: Open any X URL (post, longform article, or same-author thread) and return a unified markdown rendering plus media + linked-page URLs. Auto-detects kind from URL + DOM. Use this for any read-only `/<handle>/status/<id>` or `/<handle>/article/<id>` URL when you want the body as markdown. Do not use to fetch timelines (`x_search_posts`, `x_profile_posts`, `x_home_timeline_digest`) or to act on a post (`x_like_post`, `x_reply_post`).

url.description: Full X URL: /<handle>/status/<id>, /<handle>/article/<id>, or any URL within a same-author thread.

mode.description: Force kind detection. Default auto picks from URL + DOM.

steps.name: Open the target URL in a fresh tab
Probe kind and redirect to /article/<id> when needed
Dismiss popups if present after redirect

help.summary: Open any X URL (post, longform article, or same-author thread) read-only and return a unified markdown rendering plus media + linked-page URLs.

parameters.mode: Force kind: auto | post | article | thread. Default auto.
parameters.url: Full X URL: /<handle>/status/<id>, /<handle>/article/<id>, or any URL within a same-author thread.

examples.description: Auto-detect kind, return markdown + asset URLs.
Force thread expansion mode regardless of detection.
Save markdown to disk and download all images + videos + linked pages locally.

returns: { kind: 'post'|'article'|'thread', url, requested_url, title, subtitle, author, author_handle, posted_at, markdown, image_urls[], video_urls[], external_links[], asset_urls[], posts?, total_posts?, resolved_via }.
notes: Read-only. Never posts, replies, or likes.
Auto-detects: /article/<id> -> article; /status/<id> with article-card -> article (auto-redirects); /status/<id> with 2+ same-author posts in main column -> thread; otherwise post.
For longform articles with the input URL in /status/ form, the page is redirected to /article/<id> mid-workflow via window.location.assign.

Use `--output-file <path>` (CLI flag) to write the markdown body to disk; use `--download-dir <dir>` to download all images, videos, and linked pages to a folder with a manifest.json mapping URL -> local path.
